source.title: Brak i biznis nisu sPRrint nego štafeta | Jana i Lovro Radeljak u PSP podcastu | EP 13

summary.title_hr: Brak i biznis nisu sPRrint nego štafeta | Jana i Lovro Radeljak u PSP podcastu | EP 13
summary.abstract_hr: U ovoj epizodi podcasta "Poduzetništvo s povjerenjem", Jana i Lovro Radeljak, bračni i poduzetnički par te vlasnici PR agencije Bagadodo, dijele svoja iskustva o usklađivanju poslovnog i privatnog života. Razgovaraju o izazovima pokretanja i vođenja agencije s troje djece, važnosti vjere i molitve u donošenju odluka te kako se nadopunjuju u poslu. Ističu da uspjeh definiraju osobno, a ne prema svjetovnim mjerilima, te nude savjete parovima koji razmišljaju o zajedničkom poduzetništvu.
key_points: Mit je da bračni parovi koji rade zajedno provode 24 sata dnevno skupa; zapravo se često izmjenjuju i nadopunjuju u različitim aktivnostima.
Jana je pokrenula agenciju Bagadodo s 25 godina, a Lovro joj se pridružio osam godina kasnije, donoseći tehničke vještine i prodajno iskustvo.
U njihovom poslovnom tandemu, Jana je više zadužena za organizaciju i planiranje, dok je Lovro operativac i logističar.
Vjera i molitva su ključni elementi u njihovom svakodnevnom životu i poslovanju, pomažući im u donošenju odluka i suočavanju s izazovima.
Uspjeh definiraju osobno, kao usklađenost svih životnih poziva (supruga, majka, poduzetnica), a ne prema svjetovnim mjerilima poput veličine tvrtke ili dobiti.
Svjesno su odlučili ne zapošljavati stalne djelatnike kako bi zadržali fleksibilnost, mogli usporiti kada obiteljske obveze to zahtijevaju i fokusirati se na kvalitetu usluge.
Savjetuju bračnim parovima koji žele pokrenuti posao da budu hrabri, realni, uporni i marljivi, te da vjeruju u Božju providnost, unatoč komentarima okoline.
summary.title_en: Marriage and business are not a sPRrint but a relay race | Jana and Lovro Radeljak in PSP podcast | EP 13
summary.abstract_en: In this episode of the podcast "Entrepreneurship with Trust", Jana and Lovro Radeljak, a married and entrepreneurial couple and owners of the PR agency Bagadodo, share their experiences on balancing business and private life. They discuss the challenges of starting and running an agency with three children, the importance of faith and prayer in decision-making, and how they complement each other in business. They emphasize that they define success personally, not according to worldly standards, and offer advice to couples considering joint entrepreneurship.
key_points_en: It is a myth that married couples who work together spend 24 hours a day together; in fact, they often alternate and complement each other in different activities.
Jana started the Bagadodo agency at 25 years old, and Lovro joined her eight years later, bringing technical skills and sales experience.
In their business tandem, Jana is more responsible for organization and planning, while Lovro is the operative and logistician.
Faith and prayer are key elements in their daily life and business, helping them in making decisions and facing challenges.
They define success personally, as the alignment of all life callings (wife, mother, entrepreneur), and not according to worldly criteria like company size or profit.
They consciously decided not to employ permanent staff in order to maintain flexibility, be able to slow down when family obligations require it, and focus on the quality of service.
They advise married couples who want to start a business to be brave, realistic, persistent, and diligent, and to believe in God's providence, despite the comments of the surroundings.
